mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS signals easier policy and lower discount rates, which lifts equity valuations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal disappears when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, labor-market deterioration implies falling household income expectations and weaker risk appetite, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative median 20-day returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad demand resilience in consumer spending and housing activity, which supports cyclically sensitive earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST do not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in the top quartile of combined demand growth.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in INDPRO signals rising industrial output and improving utilization, which confirms stronger real activity and should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month INDPRO gains fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the relation turns negative in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% and CPIAUCSL is still rising, restrictive policy coincides with unresolved inflation pressure, compressing real liquidity and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate periods with rising CPIAUCSL do not yield negative median 20-day returns, or if the interaction adds no predictive power beyond FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDPC1 reflects weakening aggregate demand and lower expected corporate revenue, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60-day forward returns, or if the sign remains positive across successive recession-like episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, falling PAYEMS signals accelerating labor-market deterioration that directly weakens consumption and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS declines under high UNRATE do not precede negative 5-day returns, or if the interaction is weaker than the standalone PAYEMS effect.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts HOUST rise after a prior contraction, the signal reflects improving rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound episodes do not produce positive 60-day returns, or if the effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is falling.
